item.title: Everyone should learn about this
item.description:
*we also created other smaller Nuclear plants as well but Bruce is the largest* they all contributed to displacing Coal
There is a lot to be said about climate change as well. But I think a lot of the time we focus on Climate change and not the direct impacts to health in cities like Toronto - Many argue that Geography plays a role in their emissions as well. Ontario has some natural advantages with Hydro but we also have a lot of disadvantages like hard winters that make renewables less viable. Every region has advantages and disadvantages.

item.title: Sunday Dump of amazing and funny things. Enjoy !
item.description:
1. Tossed him like a ragdoll
2. She's a fairy.
3. Great age and treachery.
4. Short man passing through turn gate.
5. Now that's a gaming setup.
6. Fun Woodpecker Bell.
7. Some sort of race with wooden blocks. She's really good at it.
8. Did you lock your keys inside ?
9. Nice catch.
10. She encountered a fairy .
11. This Hydraulic Tripod.
12. Learn Morse code in 45 seconds !
13. The coolest restaurant in Japan.
14. Slate roof tiles.
15. A demonstration of how to untangle using topology.
16. This man build a LEGO city.
17. This is business.
18. How Fake Money Is Cut with Precision.
19. Don't feed beans to ducks.
20. RC Car Football.
21. A breathtaking view of Antarctica with sunlit ice and endless horizons.
22. A bartender in India.
23. Amazing Landing.
24. Valve seats installation.
25. Shopping cart washer.
26. The Builder.
27. Sometimes, you gotta do what you gotta do.
28. Physics and art make for a powerful combination.
29. How incredible for the kids to be welcomed by their idols.
30. Airlifting of high power electrical transmission tower.
31. Tree Hugger In Action.
32. Indian man performs an underwater pendulum.
33. Those guys show how magic is done.
34. WTF with that reaction ?
35. Shut up and take my money.
36. A craftsman from Vietnam has assembled an unusual car.

item.title: Lil meme dump for your Sunday <3
item.description:
Actually suits their characters so well
Looks innocent to me, officer
This had me cracking up...
@danat101 I made this a while ago but never posted it. danatello is less active these days (which my limited lifetime is thankful for <3)
Food baby is best baby
If you cant laugh about farts I feel bad for you
I tried taking a picture of a fox I saw outside but something was wrong with the camera, and now it looks like art
Goals
Truth
It be like that
Some guys I know would pay money for that
:o
I need to tidy up... I feel called out.
Cat tax <3 Have a lovely Sunday night!

item.dc:creator: annmiha

item.title: Budapest just held the largest pride in history despite police ban
item.description:
I wanted to share what happened yesterday at Budapest Pride, because I don't see enough coverage for it, even though it was a HUGE win for my country, a beautiful day to show that there is always hope and I'm happy that I was part of it! We showed the world what it truly means to be an ally!
In March, Orban's government passed a law that allowed the ban of pride, posing as a "child protection law". Organizers of the Budapest Pride vowed to continue with the annual festival despite the new law and declaring: “We will fight this new fascist ban.”
A few weeks ago, an official ban was issued: organizing or attending could lead to legal consequences, including a 500 euro fine and possible criminal charges.
The mayor of Budapest, who's openly opposed to Orban's regime, showed support and announced that he's working with the organizers to pass the event as a protest, which made the police ban invalid under the rights of assembly.
But the police ban remained in effect. Facial recognition was deployed to identify participants along the planned route of the parade. The use of drones was banned except for police surveillance ones (a big part of why you don't see air footage showing the real size of gatherings). Additionally, far-right groups received permits to hold counter-demonstrations at the same time, blocking a bridge that was part of the planned route.
They did everything to scare us, and they failed. The crowd of around 200 000 people marched on, it was the largest pride in our history. It was an incredible experience to march in a crowd as large as 1/10th of the city. The pictures that exist don't do it justice. It was heartwarming to see how many people are willing to stand up for democracy and the protection of queer people. I finally see a slim hope of dismantling this 15 years old regime on our back!

item.dc:creator: ewakemanVA

item.title: Why are all kilts wool? It's hot AF in Michigan
item.description: I'm a man 32 and an ally to our queer friends. Let a few people know I was going to go look for a skirt (because it's a trillion fucking degrees out) without the shorts built in for around the house. Ended up getting way more push back than I ever expected. I am the type of person that doesn't tend to care what other people think of me but it was still surprising. I will say I didn't not find one and I'm going to continue my hunting. But to whomever is reading this that doesn't have confidence to wear whatever they would like you do have friends out there. Not everyone was a giant dickhead. I'm here to chat with. Also suggestions for lighter fabric and bigger sized brands are welcomed. Also I'm reminded of when Prince was asked: "Are you wearing women's clothes?" he replied "No I'm wearing my clothes". Stay cool and hydrated everybody. Kisses
item.dc:creator: IfILikeYourJokeITellYou

item.title: how soon people forget
item.description: “History doesn't repeat itself but it often rhymes.” When someone shows you who they are, believe them the first time. As they say, give someone an inch and they'll take a mile. Keep sane-washing an batshit crazy presidency on top of that, and what do you get? Chaos beyond belief. The supreme court just expanded Trump's power. Barring judges from swiftly blocking government actions, even when they may be illegal. Curtailing nationwide injunctions against Trump's order ending birthright citizenship. And the administration just terminated legal status for more than 500,000 immigrants. As George Carlin, pointed out: "In 1942 there were 110,000 Japanese-American citizens, in good standing, law abiding people, who were thrown into internment camps simply because their parents were born in the wrong country. Just when these American citizens needed their rights the most, their government took them away." The White House press secretary has been compared to North Korean state media. A congressmember has asked Attorney General Pam Bondi to get Zohran Mamdani, winner of the Democratic primary for NYC Mayor, denaturalized. All because of differing political beliefs. At least three federal prosecutors who worked on cases against January 6 rioters were fired by the Justice Department, “removed from federal service effective immediately” - no reason for the removal stated. The parallels are undeniable. It's a disgrace that so many old-guards in the Democrat establishment (see Chuck Schumer) seem disinterested in fighting harder against the corrupt GOP. Just bending over again and again. If the rights of American citizens are not secure, if no one is totally safe, it won't take long before before things get even more drastic. Desperate times? Desperate measures.... "Those who make peaceful revolution impossible will make violent revolution inevitable."
item.dc:creator: trojanbarfare
